summary: Acromegaly is caused by excessive growth hormone secretion after epiphyseal fusion, leading to abnormal soft tissue and skeletal enlargement due to lack of further linear growth potential. This distinguishes it from gigantism, where growth happens prior to epiphyseal closure.

This distinguishes it from gigantism, where growth happens prior to epiphyseal closure.. Answer choice: B
